docs.title: Cook The Flag
docs.text: Cook The Flag is a web-based CTF tool that lets you easily extend its functionality with Python-based plugins. It simplifies the use of various CTF tools and techniques, making it easier to tackle a wide range of challenges, from web exploitation to cryptography, and more. The project is designed to grow over time with new features and capabilities.

docs.title: 📃 Features
docs.text:
• 🧮 Basic Encryption (Base64, Hex, ...)
  • 🔨 Tools (Requests, NMap, ...)
  • 🔍 RegEx search
  • 🐳 Docker image

docs.title: 🌠 In the future
docs.text:
• ➕ More tools (RsaCtfTool, ...)
  • 🔧 More conversions (Rot13, ...)
  • 🌐 Remote access

docs.title: 🚀 Usage
docs.text:
curl -s https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zalo-alex/CookTheFlag/refs/heads/main/run.sh | bash
Other methods (Latest updates) Using DockerHub 
docker run --pull=always --volume ~/.cooktheflag:/data -p 8080:8080 azalo/cooktheflag
Using Dockerfile 
git clone https://github.com/zalo-alex/CookTheFlag
cd CookTheFlag
sudo docker build -t cooktheflag .
sudo docker run --volume ~/.cooktheflag:/data -p 8080:8080 cooktheflag
Using Python 
git clone https://github.com/zalo-alex/CookTheFlag
cd CookTheFlag
pip install -r requirements.txt
python app.py

docs.title: 👤 Contributions
docs.text: You can make your own modules and share them with a pull request

docs.title: Getting started
docs.text:
• File Structure
  • Modules
  • Layout
  • RegEx
  • Parsers

docs.title: File Structure
docs.text:
Here are the important files if you want to create modules: 

.
├─ modules/
└─ src/
   ├─ layout/
   ├─ parsers/
   └─ regexs.py

docs.title: modules/
docs.text: This folder contains all the modules that will be loaded, you cannot use subfolders.

docs.title: src/layout/
docs.text: This folder contains all the graphic elements that can be used in the modules. All the elements should be in the __all__ of __init__.py.

docs.title: src/parsers/
docs.text: This folder contains all the parsers that can be used in the modules, all the elements are in the __all__ of __init__.py.

docs.title: src/regexs.py
docs.text:
This file contains all the regexes that can be used in the modules.

Page is currently in work in progress

docs.title: Input
docs.text:
Input(name, id, type="text", placeholder="", value="", textarea=False, regex = r"", parser = None)

This element is a <input> in HTML.

name

The label, only visual

id

The actual ID to retrieve and edit values

type, placeholder, value

Same as HTML

textarea

If true it will use a <textarea> instead of an <input>

regex

The RegEx that will redirect to the input when a search is done

parser

More infos Parsers

docs.title: Submit
docs.text:
Button(name, id, auto=False)

The button to submit the data

name

The label, only visual

id

The type in the submit function

auto

Only for client side, will execute submit every an input is modified

Page is currently in work in progress

docs.title: Modules
docs.text:
Here's a basic model of what a module is

modules/hello.py
from src.module import Module
from src.layout import *

class CustomModule(Module):
    name = "Say Hello"
    category = "random-utils"
    layout = [
        Input("Your name", "name-input"),
        Submit("Say", "say"),
        Input("Response", "response")
    ]

    def submit(type, data):
        return {
            "response": "Hello " + data["name-input"]
        }

category

Should be in lowercase with '-' instead of spaces

layout

A list of Layout elements, the Submit will trigger the submit function with his type (here say)

submit(type, data)

type is the type of the submitter data a dict with all the contents of the inputs, the key is the ID of the element It returns a dict with each content to change, element ID and value. There are no timeouts

docs.title: Client side
docs.text:
By adding a script attribute to CustomModule you can make your module client side. You will have to make it in JavaScript, the type and data are already initialized, no need to create a function

from src.module import Module
from src.layout import *

class CustomModule(Module):
    ...

    script = """return {
        "response": "Hello " + data["name-input"]
    }"""

docs.title: Module RegEx
docs.text:
With the regex you can specify an expression for the module and not only to an input. It can be usefull if the user want to know the type of string he's searching. For more info about RegEx see RegEx

from src.regexs import Regexs

class CustomModule(Module):
    name = "Base64 Encode"
    category = "encoding"
    regex=Regexs.BASE64

docs.title: Streaming response
docs.text:
I you need modify multiple times your response, for exemple with CLI, you can use yield instead of return

class CustomModule(Module):
    ...

    def submit(type, data):
        for i in range(10):
            yield {
                "result": i
            }
            time.sleep(1)

Page is currently in work in progress
